description: Byte-level pubkey validation. Each `child_ed25519_pub` / `child_x25519_pub` MUST base64url-decode to EXACTLY 32 bytes. A mere string-length check is insufficient (the PR #64 gap: a ≥43-char string that is not 32 decoded bytes). Every case below MUST be REJECTED (BAD_PUBKEY) — the endpoint does not parse attestation or pin.

reject_pubkeys.why: decodes to far fewer than 32 bytes
44 chars → 33 decoded bytes (the ≈32.25-byte over-long blob PR #64 accepted)
trailing '=' padding is not allowed in unpadded base64url
standard-base64 '+'/'/' are outside the url-safe alphabet

expect: REJECTED for every entry (BAD_PUBKEY); the session is NOT burned by a malformed POST
